name: Philepittidae
description: Asities are small, brightly colored passerines endemic to Madagascar. They are closely related to the broadbills and were once considered part of that family.

children.name: Philepitta
children.description: Philepitta is a genus of asities endemic to Madagascar, known for their fleshy wattles around the eyes.

children.name: Philepitta castanea
children.description: The velvet asity is a plump, short-tailed bird found in eastern Madagascar rainforests. Males have striking black velvety plumage with bright green iridescence and blue wattles.

children.name: Philepitta schlegeli
children.description: Schlegel's asity is restricted to the dry forests of western Madagascar. The male has mostly yellow plumage with a black patch and blue facial wattles.

children.name: Neodrepanis
children.description: Neodrepanis is a genus of asities with long, curved bills adapted for nectar feeding, convergent with sunbirds.

children.name: Neodrepanis coruscans
children.description: The sunbird asity is a small nectar-feeding bird of Madagascar's eastern rainforests. Its long decurved bill and bright blue facial wattles make it unmistakable.

children.name: Neodrepanis hypoxantha
children.description: The yellow-bellied sunbird asity is a vulnerable species endemic to montane forests of eastern Madagascar. It has a bright yellow belly and the characteristic wattles of the genus.
